title: lepidotis inundata
intro: Lepidotis inundata, also known as marsh fleabane, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Lepidotis inundata include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Lepidotis inundata can be prepared for medicinal use in various ways, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
